articles.title: Gardening in Cold Weather: Our Year-Round Growing Strategy
articles.description: We grow food 10 months of the year in zone 6b. Here is our cold-weather gardening system, the crops that work, and the temperature data from three years of trial and error.

articles.title: The Best Websites for Weather Information
articles.description: We tested 12 weather websites over 18 months. These are the ones we actually use for planning off-grid operations, storm tracking, and long-term forecasting.

articles.title: Composting with Earthworms: The Vermicomposting System That Works
articles.description: We process 5 pounds of kitchen scraps per week into rich compost using earthworms. Here is our working worm bin setup and the lessons from three years.

articles.title: Cleaning Solar Panels Once a Year
articles.description: We clean our solar panels once per year. Here is the system that works, the tools, the technique, and the data on power loss from dirty panels.

articles.title: The Best Aromatic Flowers to Plant
articles.description: We planted 15 varieties of aromatic flowers over three years. These are the ones that actually produced scent, survived our winters, and fit an off-grid lifestyle.

articles.title: Cold Frames: Extend Your Growing Season by Weeks
articles.description: We harvested lettuce from our cold frames on March 15 this year — six weeks before the last frost. We picked kale through December 28. Here is how cold frames work, three build designs, and the temperature data.

articles.title: DIY Solar Water Pumping: Stream to Tank Without Electricity
articles.description: 300 gallons per day pumped from our stream to a 1,000-gallon storage tank using a Shurflo 9300 diaphragm pump and a 160W solar panel. Full component list ($754 total), sizing math, and two years of operating data.

articles.title: Building a Simple Root Cellar: Modern Budget Construction
articles.description: An 8x10 concrete block root cellar built into a north-facing slope, $1,211 all in, holding 400 pounds of produce at 33–42°F through winter. Complete materials list, ventilation system, and 12 months of temperature data.

articles.title: Best Water Filters for Off-Grid Living in 2026
articles.description: We tested eight water filters over 18 months. From $20 bucket filters to $1,200 whole-house systems — these are the ones that actually work in off-grid conditions.

articles.title: Best Wood Stoves for Off-Grid Living in 2026
articles.description: We tested eight wood stoves over four winters. From rocket mass heaters to catalytic converters — these are the ones that actually heat a cabin efficiently.

articles.title: Best Solar Generators for Off-Grid Living in 2026
articles.description: We tested five solar generators over six months. From portable units to whole-home systems — these are the ones that actually perform in real off-grid conditions.

articles.title: Best Water Filters in 2026
articles.description: We tested seven water filters including Berkey, Lifestraw, and Alexapure. These are the ones that actually remove pathogens and heavy metals.

articles.title: Best Solar Panels for Off-Grid Living in 2026
articles.description: Five panels tested in real-world conditions: Renogy, ECO-WORTHY, Newpowa, Rich Solar, and SunPower. Ranked by real output, cost per watt, and build quality.

articles.title: Building a DIY Greenhouse on a Budget
articles.description: Four greenhouse designs from $200 to $600 using common materials. Siting, passive heating, ventilation, and which design works best in cold climates.

articles.title: Building a Root Cellar: Complete DIY Guide
articles.description: Five root cellar designs from $200 to $2,000. Site selection, temperature and humidity targets, ventilation, and what to store and when. The original no-electricity food storage system.

articles.title: How We Built Our Gravity-Fed Water System for $400
articles.description: No pump, no electricity, no pressure tank. A 500-gallon hilltop tank feeds water to the cabin by gravity alone. Full build log with materials list, costs, and what we'd do differently.

articles.title: Building a DIY Outdoor Shower
articles.description: A simple outdoor shower for rinsing off after work or play. Solar-powered with a 5-gallon bag, drain field, and privacy. $85 in materials, 2 hours to build.

articles.title: Making Biochar on Your Property
articles.description: Three years of making biochar from our own wood waste. A $28 TLUD drum build, three production methods compared, charging protocols, and application rates that move the needle in garden beds.

articles.title: Off-Grid Food Storage Without Refrigeration
articles.description: Canning, dehydrating, root cellaring, and fermenting. How we store a year's worth of food without electricity, including costs per quart and shelf life data.

articles.title: LiFePO4 vs AGM Batteries for Off-Grid
articles.description: We compared LiFePO4 and AGM batteries over 18 months. Cycle life, efficiency, depth of discharge, and real-world costs per kilowatt-hour.

articles.title: Building a Cob Oven: Complete Guide
articles.description: A wood-fired cob oven for baking and cooking. Materials list, build process, and thermal mass data from five years of use.

articles.title: Rainwater Harvesting: Complete System Guide
articles.description: From gutters to cisterns — how we harvest and store rainwater. Sizing calculations, filtration options, and 12 months of collection data.

articles.title: Solar System Sizing: The Math
articles.description: How to calculate exactly what solar system you need. Daily usage, panel output, battery capacity, and inverter sizing – with actual examples from our 2.4kW system.

articles.title: Building a Tiny Cabin: Complete Guide
articles.description: Our 200sqft cabin build. Foundation, framing, insulation, and utilities – with costs, timelines, and lessons learned from building it ourselves.

articles.title: How to Conduct a Power Audit
articles.description: How to determine exactly how much power you use – and what you need to generate. A step-by-step process with worksheets.

articles.title: Water Filtration Guide for Off-Grid
articles.description: What you need to filter, what each filter removes, and how to build a complete system for under $500.
